metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the docs/api-stability.md edit target: confirm whether a Python-version table/statement should be *added* (and specify what it should say) or remove api-stability.md from the file list if it has no version wording to update.
findings.finding: docs/api-stability.md contains zero Python-version statements — there is no 'supported-versions wording' in that file to update. The plan names it as an edit target, but the edit surface does not exist; any update would require adding new content (CREATION), which is out of scope, or the file reference is a hallucination.
findings.location: What / files section — 'docs/api-stability.md / README supported-versions wording updated to match'; also AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Remove docs/api-stability.md from the edit list (it has no Python-version content to update); the README's '≥ 3.11' floor wording and the pyproject classifiers are the only docs artifacts that need touching.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that docs/api-stability.md has no existing Python-version wording to update (it covers API surface stability, not interpreter support); identify the actual file(s) that enumerate supported Python versions (README.md uses a floor `≥ 3.11`, not an explicit list) and specify precisely what text to add/change in each.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'docs/api-stability.md / README supported-versions wording updated to match' — implying docs/api-stability.md currently contains Python-version wording to update. A full read of that file shows it contains zero Python version numbers; it covers API surface stability (schemas, CLI, Python facade) but has no interpreter-support declarations. There is no existing supported-version statement in api-stability.md to update.
findings.location: What / files section: 'docs/api-stability.md / README supported-versions wording updated to match'; AC: 'README/docs supported-version statements match the matrix'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'docs/api-stability.md' in the What/files section with the specific location that should gain 3.13 coverage. README.md currently says '≥ 3.11' at two points (lines 140 and 193) — if the intent is to keep a floor statement, those lines may need no change at all since `≥ 3.11` already includes 3.13. If the intent is to add an explicit enumeration, call that out and name the target file. Remove docs/api-stability.md from scope unless a new 'Supported Python versions' section is intentionally being added there.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what 'README supported-versions wording updated to match' means concretely: is the ≥3.11 floor sufficient once 3.13 is tested, or should a per-version list be added? If no change is needed, remove README from the edit list.
findings.finding: The README does not have a dedicated 'supported-versions' section — it states 'Python ≥ 3.11' as a floor (lines 140, 193) but never enumerates 3.11/3.12 individually. The plan's AC4 ('README/docs supported-version statements match the matrix') and the What/files line 'README supported-versions wording updated to match' imply a section exists that can be updated, but the actual content to change is unspecified.
findings.location: What / files section — 'README supported-versions wording'; AC4
findings.suggested_fix: If the README's ≥3.11 floor already implies 3.13 support, document that no README change is needed. If an explicit list is desired, specify the exact wording to add (e.g. 'Tested on Python 3.11, 3.12, 3.13').
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the other workflows that pin python-version: "3.12" (external-integration.yml, release.yml, optionality.yml, eval.yml, eval-plan-review-container.yml, mirror-guard.yml) and explicitly state whether they are in or out of scope for the 3.13 addition.
findings.finding: The plan enumerates only the test matrix cells in test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml, but at least six other workflows hard-pin python-version: "3.12" as non-matrix single values: external-integration.yml, release.yml, optionality.yml, eval.yml, eval-plan-review-container.yml, and mirror-guard.yml. These are unenumerated consumers that, while not requiring 3.13 themselves, represent a set of jobs that consistently run only on 3.12 — and the plan does not acknowledge them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the standalone `lint + type-check` job in test.yml (and its mirror in gerrit-verify.yaml) should also be updated to run on 3.13 (or confirm that running it only on 3.12 is an accepted cost policy, and document that decision alongside the existing macOS cost-policy note).
findings.finding: The plan adds 3.13 only to the matrix jobs but leaves the standalone `lint + type-check` job in test.yml (and `require-ticket`/`external` in gerrit-verify.yaml) hardcoded to python-version: '3.12'. This means CI never runs mypy or ruff against a 3.13 interpreter, so any 3.13-specific type-checker or linter regression is not caught by a gate — the plan's AC3 ('make lint && make typecheck … green under a local 3.13 interpreter') relies on a local check rather than a CI-enforced gate for this tier.
findings.location: .github/workflows/test.yml — `lint` job (line 34-79, pinned to '3.12'); plan section 'What / files'
findings.suggested_fix: Either bump the lint job's `python-version` to `"3.13"` once the matrix is green (making 3.13 the gating interpreter for lint/mypy, consistent with '3.13 is current-stable'), or add an explicit note in the plan that the lint job deliberately stays on 3.12 for cost/stability reasons and local 3.13 verification (AC3) is the accepted substitute.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC4, e.g. `grep -r '3\.13' README.md docs/api-stability.md` (or equivalent), so the docs update is mechanically verifiable.
findings.finding: AC4 ('README/docs supported-version statements match the matrix') has no concrete proving command — the executor has no deterministic check, only a manual visual comparison with no specified artifact anchor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — last bullet: 'README/docs supported-version statements match the matrix'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Verified by: `grep -r "3\.13" README.md docs/api-stability.md` exits 0 with matches in the supported-versions section of each file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default artifact for the blocker documentation (e.g., 'a comment block in pyproject.toml under the classifiers section and a note in CHANGELOG') so the executor has a defined target and reviewers have a predictable location to verify.
findings.finding: The conditional remedy branch 'if a real blocker exists, document why 3.13 is excluded instead' does not specify the target artifact for the documentation, leaving the executor to choose an appropriate location.
findings.location: What / files — 'Fix any 3.13-specific failures … or, if a real blocker exists, document why 3.13 is excluded instead'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'Fix any 3.13-specific failures' bullet: 'If excluded, document the rationale in a `[tool.project-status]` comment in pyproject.toml and a CHANGELOG entry; this constitutes the verifiable artifact for AC3's blocker branch.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the hard-coded `matrix.python-version == '3.12'` pip-audit condition in gerrit-verify.yaml so it continues to run on exactly one cell (e.g. the highest version, or ubuntu+3.13) after 3.13 is added to the matrix.
findings.finding: Within gerrit-verify.yaml, the require-ticket job (line 77) and the pip-audit conditional (line 245: `matrix.python-version == '3.12'`) are unenumerated consumers of the python-version value. The pip-audit gate will silently not run on 3.13 matrix cells after the matrix expansion, because the condition hard-codes '3.12'.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the condition to `matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest' && matrix.python-version == '3.13'` (or whichever cell should carry the audit) once 3.13 is the highest version in the matrix.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
